macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $65,000 Amid Trump Tariff Uncertainty
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped 4.8% to $64,300, its lowest level since February 6, as Trump's tariff policy changes rattled markets. Spot Bitcoin ETFs have experienced $3.8 billion in outflows over five consecutive weeks, with analysts identifying $60,000 as the next critical support level.

items.title: Ethereum Falls 5.2% as Vitalik Buterin Sells Holdings
items.summary: Ethereum declined 5.2% to $1,878.63 after reports showed founder Vitalik Buterin sold at least 1,694 Ether worth $3.3 million over the weekend. Other major altcoins including XRP, Solana, and Cardano also posted sharp declines between 3-8%.

items.title: Crypto Market Loses $100 Billion in 24 Hours
items.summary: The broader cryptocurrency market shed $100 billion in value over the past day, with over $2 trillion wiped out overall since the start of 2026. Bitcoin is down 25% year-to-date amid macro uncertainty from geopolitical tensions and tariff policy.

items.title: South Korea Prepares Strict Crypto Regulations Following Bithumb's $40 Billion Error
items.summary: Lawmakers are set to crack down on cryptocurrency exchanges with regulations mirroring traditional banking standards, following Bithumb's accidental distribution of $40 billion in Bitcoin to customers in February. The Digital Asset Taskforce will meet on February 24 to discuss new regulatory frameworks.

items.title: Dragonfly Raises $650 Million Fourth Fund Amid VC Ecosystem Concerns
items.summary: Prominent crypto venture firm Dragonfly closed a $650 million fourth fund despite predictions of a 'mass extinction event' in the VC ecosystem. The firm's portfolio includes Polymarket and Ethena.

items.title: BlockFills Halts Operations After $75 Million Loss
items.summary: Susquehanna-backed crypto lender BlockFills suspended deposits and withdrawals after losing at least $75 million during the recent market downturn, echoing concerns from the 2022 crypto crisis.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Pew Research Releases Report on U.S. Teens' Use and Views of AI
items.summary: Just over half of U.S. teens report using chatbots for schoolwork, with 12% relying on them regularly; teens are divided on AI's future impact, citing benefits like efficiency alongside concerns over job loss and overreliance.

items.title: Cisco Finds Open-Weight AI Models Vulnerable to 93% Jailbreak Success Rate
items.summary: Cisco tested eight major open-weight AI models, revealing that multi-turn jailbreak attacks succeeded nearly 93% of the time, highlighting significant safety risks in these systems.

items.title: White House Launches Global AI Initiatives Including 'American AI Stack' and U.S. Tech Corps
items.summary: The White House is advancing its AI action plan with export-ready AI technology packages, a new U.S. Tech Corps, and standards initiatives to promote American leadership in AI.

items.title: IBM Showcases High-NA EUV Advances for Sub-2nm Chips at SPIE 2026 Conference
items.summary: IBM Research presented a roadmap for High NA EUV lithography enabling scaling below 2nm nodes, including polarization-engineered imaging and process modules for AI-era semiconductor manufacturing.

items.title: FSU Hosts 2026 AI and Machine Learning Expo Focusing on Agentic AI
items.summary: Florida State University's AIMLX26 expo on February 27-28 explores agentic AI applications in medicine, finance, and education, featuring OpenAI's Sherwin Wu as keynote on AI agent trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Microsoft Appoints New Gaming CEO Asha Sharma as Phil Spencer Steps Down
items.summary: Microsoft has named Asha Sharma, former Instacart and Meta executive, as the new CEO of Xbox and Microsoft Gaming following Phil Spencer's retirement after nearly 40 years. Sharma vows to revitalize Xbox hardware and services with AI integration while emphasizing human-crafted games amid recent revenue declines.

items.title: Nintendo Announces Treehouse Live Showcasing Pokémon Pokopia and Super Mario Bros. Wonder Switch 2 Edition
items.summary: Nintendo will host a Treehouse Live on February 24 at 5 PM ET featuring hands-on gameplay of Pokémon Pokopia and the Switch 2 edition of Super Mario Bros. Wonder, which includes new DLC with Bellabel Park and additional characters. Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen Versions are also coming digitally to Switch eShop post-Pokémon Presents.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Launches as 2026's Most Anticipated Blockbuster
items.summary: Capcom's Resident Evil Requiem, the latest mainline survival-horror entry, releases this week amid massive hype, high pre-orders, and praise for its psychological tension, cinematic action, and next-gen visuals. It overshadows other releases like Nested Lands and Tales of Berseria Remastered.

items.title: Video Game Stocks to Watch: Turtle Beach, Motorsport Games, and Others Highlighted
items.summary: MarketBeat identifies Turtle Beach, Motorsport Games, Allied Gaming & Entertainment, Alliance Entertainment, and Brag House as top video game stocks to research on February 23, amid focus on esports, racing sims, and experiential entertainment.

items.title: Gaming Industry Faces Sales Slowdown Despite Growing Player Base
items.summary: The industry grapples with declining game purchases amid a larger player base, driven by shifts to long-term 'forever games,' AI integration at Microsoft and Sony, live-service failures, and changing demographics. Calls grow for a more inclusive ecosystem.

items.title: Xsolla Co-Sponsors Gamescom Dev Leadership Summit in Lisbon
items.summary: Xsolla is co-sponsoring the Gamescom Dev Leadership Summit on February 23 to unite industry leaders on game development, monetization, and global expansion tools for over 1,500 developers worldwide.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
